ports.notes: Protocol 2.0, ECDSA and ED25519 host keys
Redirects to http://pterodactyl.htb/. Multiple vhosts: pterodactyl.htb (landing page), panel.pterodactyl.htb (Pterodactyl Panel), play.pterodactyl.htb (redirects to main)

anomalies.description: phpinfo.php exposed on main domain — reveals full PHP config, PEAR paths, disabled functions (none), open_basedir (none)
anomalies.significance: Critical information disclosure — confirms attack prerequisites for CVE-2025-49132

anomalies.description: changelog.txt publicly accessible — reveals exact software versions, installation details, and security-relevant config decisions
anomalies.significance: Operational intelligence — confirms Pterodactyl Panel v1.11.10, MariaDB 11.8.3, PHP-PEAR installed

oracle_recommendations.action: Exploit CVE-2025-49132 (Pterodactyl Panel unauthenticated LFI-to-RCE) on panel.pterodactyl.htb
oracle_recommendations.rationale: CVSS 10.0, unauthenticated, public exploit available. Target runs exact vulnerable version (1.11.10). All prerequisites confirmed: PHP-PEAR installed, register_argc_argv=On, no disable_functions, no open_basedir. PEAR path confirmed at /usr/share/php/PEAR.

oracle_recommendations.action: Extract .env credentials via LFI before RCE (Laravel APP_KEY, DB creds)
oracle_recommendations.rationale: Credential extraction may provide additional lateral movement paths and is a free byproduct of the LFI
